title: review --prepare fails when project path contains spaces
author: Vuk97

stage1.summary: The code path for review --prepare goes through setup_lang_concrete -> file_finder -> find_source_files -> _find_source_files_cached, which uses os.walk and Path operations that handle spaces fine. The scan command uses the same file_finder. The discrepancy likely comes from how the review packet context resolves path (ReviewPacketContext uses Path(args.path) directly at build.py:59) versus how the scan resolves it (prepare_scan_runtime at workflow.py:285). The scan calls resolve_lang which sets up the full runtime including project root resolution via get_project_root (Path.cwd().resolve()), while review --prepare's setup_lang_concrete gets the raw Path('.') without the same resolution chain. When CWD contains spaces, the relative-to-project-root computation in _safe_relpath or _find_source_files_cached may produce mismatched cache keys or paths. This is plausible but I cannot confirm it reproduces deterministically from the code alone -- the os.walk/Path layer handles spaces correctly, so the bug may be in a subtle interaction with relpath computation or caching. Reported against v0.9.9; current code may have shifted slightly.
conditions: Reproduce first: create a path with spaces and run both scan and review --prepare to confirm the bug still exists in 0.9.11
If confirmed, the fix should be in path normalization/resolution, not in quoting -- ensure review --prepare resolves paths through the same chain as scan
stage1.suggested_fix: Ensure review --prepare resolves the scan path through the same get_project_root/resolve chain that scan uses. The likely fix is in build.py's resolve_review_packet_context or in setup_lang_concrete to resolve the path before passing to file_finder. Add a test with a tmp directory containing a space.
